metadata.notes: Content overhaul to Wingspan standard — Rogue B, March 2026. Sources: official Stonemaier rulebook, BGG FAQ, r/boardgames strategy threads. BGG rank: 650, rating: 7.1, weight: 3.0.

key_rulings.text: The first player to place all 10 of their authority tokens wins immediately.

When you roll dice, sum the values of ALL your available (off-board) workers plus your knowledge track position. If the total is 16 or higher, you lose the available worker with the highest knowledge value.
Commodities (water, energy, food, bliss) are generated directly by worker placement. Resources (gold, stone, clay) require commodities to produce via the resource markets.
Players who contribute to building a market each place 1 authority token on it. Players who did NOT contribute suffer that market's ongoing penalty.

Each faction has an allegiance track with 4 tiers. Tier 1: +1 commodity at that faction's area. Tier 2: tunnels grant both options. Tier 3: all hidden recruits of that faction activate. Tier 4: place 1 authority star on each active recruit of that faction.
Your position on the morale track determines your hand size for artifact cards. Higher morale = more cards in hand.
The knowledge track determines your worker desertion threshold. If your workers' total knowledge exceeds this number when retrieved, you lose a worker.
When a tunnel is completed (all spaces filled by workers), each player with a worker in that tunnel places 1 authority token. All workers are then returned to their owners.
If you place a worker on a space already occupied by another player's worker, you bump their worker back to them. If you place on your own worker, you get the benefit of both dice values.
Pay 3 Energy to gain a new worker (lose 2 morale) or pay 3 Water to gain a new worker (gain 2 morale). Start with 2 workers, max 4.
If at the start of your turn you have 2+ available workers showing the same number, you may place all matching workers on that turn, resolving each individually.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Board
- 1 large game board showing 4 faction territories: Euphorian, Subterran, Wastelander, and Icarite
- Each territory has commodity generation spaces, resource markets, tunnels, and construction sites
- Central area contains shared knowledge and morale tracks

#### Worker Dice
- 24 custom dice (4 per player in 6 colors) — these dice ARE your workers
- Each face shows a knowledge level from 1 to 6
- The number showing when you place a worker is that worker's knowledge value

#### Cards
- 48 recruit cards — each belongs to 1 of the 4 factions with a special ability; you start with 2 recruits (1 face-up/active, 1 face-down/activates later via allegiance)
- 36 artifact cards — collected from board spaces and turned in as sets to place authority tokens
- Ethical dilemma cards — one-time-use choices you trigger during play

#### Tokens & Markers
- 60 authority tokens (10 per player, star-shaped) — place all 10 to win
- Commodity tokens — Water (blue), Energy (yellow), Food (brown), Bliss (purple); generated by placing workers on commodity spaces
- Resource tokens — Gold (yellow bars), Stone (gray blocks), Clay (red bricks); produced at resource markets by spending commodities
- 6 knowledge markers and 6 morale markers (1 each per player, placed on their respective tracks)

#### Markets & Structures
- 6 double-sided market tiles — placed on construction sites; contributors place an authority token, non-contributors suffer ongoing penalties

#### Other
- 1 progress token for tracking allegiance
- 1 rulebook (12 pages)
- GameTrayz insert

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Setup
subtopics.content:
#### How the Game Works
- **Worker-placement game** — your workers are dice, racing to place all **10 authority tokens** first
- **Knowledge levels** — the number on each die is that worker's knowledge; too-smart workers desert
- **Core tension** — keep workers dumb enough to stay while using them effectively

#### The 4 Factions
- **Euphorians** — city dwellers who generate **energy** (yellow lightning)
- **Subterrans** — underground miners who generate **water** (blue drops)
- **Wastelanders** — scrapyard survivors who generate **food** (brown)
- **Icarites** — cloud-city elites who generate **bliss** (purple clouds)
- Each faction controls its own territory with unique commodity spaces and mechanics

#### Setting Up
1. **Place the board** — stock commodity and resource tokens in their supply areas
2. **Grab starting pieces** — 2 worker dice in your color, 10 authority tokens, 1 knowledge marker, 1 morale marker
3. **Roll your 2 starting dice** — add their values together and place your knowledge marker at that total on the knowledge track
4. **Deal 4 recruit cards** — choose 2 to keep (return others unseen); place 1 face-up (active immediately) and 1 face-down (activates later via faction allegiance)
5. **Draw artifact cards** — equal to your morale track hand size
6. **Place market tiles** — randomly on the construction sites
7. **Choose a starting player**

#### Checkpoint
- 2 worker dice per player (rolled, numbers visible)
- 10 authority tokens in your color
- Markers on both knowledge and morale tracks
- 1 face-up recruit, 1 face-down recruit
- Artifact cards in hand equal to morale-based hand size
- Board: commodity areas stocked, construction sites with market tiles, resource markets ready

subtopics.title: Turn Flow
subtopics.content:
#### Your Turn
- On your turn, do exactly **one** of two things: **Place a Worker** or **Retrieve Workers**

#### Option A: Place a Worker
- **Basic action** — pick up 1 available worker die and place it on an action space; immediately resolve that space's effect
- **Bumping** — if you place on a bumpable space where an opponent's worker sits, their worker returns to them (they must roll it and perform a knowledge check); you still get the space's benefit
- **Doubles rule** — at the start of your turn, if 2+ available workers show the **same number**, you may place all matching workers that turn, resolving each individually

#### Placement Spaces
- **Commodity areas** (Generator, Aquifer, Farm, Cloud Mine) — gain commodities; benefit scales with total knowledge of all workers on that space (low total = 1 commodity + allegiance advance; medium = 1 commodity; high = 2 commodities)
- **Construction sites** — contribute a resource to build a market; workers needed depends on player count (2-3p: 2 of 4 spaces; 4p: 3 of 4; 5-6p: all 4); when complete, every contributor places an authority token
- **Tunnels** — place worker + pay a resource to advance the miner meeple; gain a resource or artifact card; at milestones, faction allegiance bonuses trigger
- **Worker Activation Tank** — pay 3 Energy (lose 2 morale) or 3 Water (gain 2 morale) to gain a new worker die; start with 2, max 4
- **Artifact markets** — pay 3 different artifact types or 2 matching artifacts to place 1 authority token
- **Constructed market spaces** — use a built market's action (only if you have a star on it)

#### Option B: Retrieve Workers
- **Action** — take one or more placed workers back into your supply, then roll all retrieved dice
- **Knowledge check (threshold: 16)** — after rolling, sum ALL available (off-board) worker dice values + your knowledge track position; if total reaches 16+, your highest-value die deserts
- **Key detail** — only off-board workers count toward the check; workers still on the board are safe

#### Core Tension
- More workers = more actions per cycle, but higher knowledge totals and greater desertion risk
- **Goal: keep knowledge low and morale high** — dumb and happy workers don't leave

subtopics.title: Economy & Factions
subtopics.content:
#### Commodities (Generated Directly)
- **Water** (blue) — from Subterran territory
- **Energy** (yellow) — from Euphorian territory
- **Food** (brown) — from Wastelander territory
- **Bliss** (purple) — from Icarite territory

#### Resources (Produced at Markets)
- **Gold** (yellow bars) — highest value
- **Stone** (gray blocks) — mid value
- **Clay** (red bricks) — most common
- **Economy flow** — place workers to earn commodities, spend commodities at resource markets to produce resources, spend resources at construction sites to build markets and place authority tokens

#### Faction Territories
- **Euphorian, Subterran, Wastelander** (ground factions) — each has commodity areas, construction sites, and tunnel connections
- **Icarites** — have commodity areas and pre-built markets but NO construction sites or tunnels; unique faction to ally with

#### Allegiance Track (4 Tiers)
- Advances as players interact with a faction's spaces; ALL players with active recruits of that faction benefit
- **Tier 1** — gain +1 commodity at that faction's commodity area
- **Tier 2** — tunnel spaces grant BOTH options (resource AND artifact card) instead of choosing one
- **Tier 3** — all hidden (face-down) recruits of that faction activate for every player who has them
- **Tier 4** — place 1 authority star on each of your active recruits of that faction (2 stars if you have 2 active recruits)

#### The Knowledge Track (1-6)
- **Adds to your knowledge check total** when retrieving workers (formula: available dice values + knowledge position; 16+ = desertion)
- **Lower is safer** — every point of knowledge makes desertion more likely
- You WANT low knowledge — smarter workers are the ones who escape

#### The Morale Track (1-6)
- **Morale = artifact card hand size** — higher morale means more flexibility for completing artifact sets
- **If morale drops** below current hand size, discard down immediately
- You WANT high morale — happy workers hold more resources for you
- **The mantra: keep your workers dumb and happy (low knowledge, high morale)**

#### Artifact Cards
- **6 types** (6 copies each, 36 total) — Teddy Bear, Baseball Bat, Balloon, Bifocals, Book, Board Game Box
- **Spending sets** — pay 3 different types or 2 matching at an artifact market to place 1 authority star

#### Ethical Dilemma Cards
- **1 per player, usable once per game** — pay 1 matching artifact or any 2 cards, then choose a path:
- **Fight the Establishment** — draw 2 recruit cards, keep 1 face-down (new hidden recruit); best early if a Tier 3 allegiance is close
- **Contribute to Dystopia** — place 1 authority star directly on your dilemma card; best in endgame when you need every star

subtopics.title: Authority Tokens & Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Ways to Place Authority Tokens
- You have **10 authority tokens** — place all 10 to win
1. **Build markets** — contribute resources to a construction site; when complete, every contributor places 1 authority token; non-contributors suffer ongoing penalties
2. **Complete tunnels** — when all spaces in a tunnel fill, every player with a worker there places 1 authority token; all workers return to owners
3. **Artifact sets** — turn in a complete set at the appropriate board space to place 1 authority token
4. **Special board spaces** — certain action spaces let you place authority tokens directly for specific costs
5. **Recruit abilities** — some recruit cards grant token placement under specific conditions

#### Market Penalties
- **Triggered on completion** — the tile flips to reveal an ongoing penalty affecting all non-contributors
- **Possible penalties** — paying extra commodities, losing a card on specific actions, being locked out of certain spaces
- **Contributors are exempt** from the penalty AND get an authority token — doubly valuable

#### Winning the Game
- **Instant victory** — the game ends immediately when any player places their 10th authority token; no final round

#### If the Game Stalls
- **Tiebreaker** — if no player can place their 10th token and progress becomes impossible, the player with the fewest remaining authority tokens wins

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Opening Priorities (Turns 1-5)
- **Generate commodities first** — place your 2 starting workers on commodity spaces to stockpile water, energy, food, or bliss
- **Focus on 1-2 faction territories** — spreading across all 4 dilutes progress; pick factions matching your face-up recruit's allegiance
- **Get a 3rd worker early** — 50% efficiency boost (3 placements per retrieve cycle instead of 2); visit the Worker Activation Tank by turn 5-7
- **Contribute to markets** — places an authority token AND protects you from the market's penalty; two-for-one value

#### Managing the Knowledge Check (Threshold: 16)
- **Formula** — available worker dice values + knowledge track position; 16+ = smartest worker deserts
- **Keep knowledge track position LOW** — a lower position gives more headroom for dice values
- **Retrieve fewer workers when possible** — only off-board workers count; leaving one on a commodity area reduces your total
- **Use bumping aggressively** — forces opponents to roll and check, potentially costing them a worker
- **Watch for doubles** — if 2+ available workers show the same number, place them all in one turn for a massive tempo swing

#### Worker Activation: When to Get Worker #3
- **Water path preferred** — pay 3 Water (gain 2 morale) vs. 3 Energy (lose 2 morale); water is strictly better
- **Aim for turn 5-7** — a 3rd worker gives a 50% efficiency boost
- **Don't rush to 4 workers** — raises knowledge check total significantly and risks constant desertions; stabilize at 3 first

#### Authority Token Priorities
1. **Markets** — best value; place a token AND avoid a penalty; always contribute if affordable
2. **Tunnels** — good value but requires coordination with other players' workers
3. **Artifact sets** — reliable but slow; need to draw and hold specific card types
4. **Special spaces** — situational; best for closing out when you need your last 1-2 tokens

#### Common Mistakes
- **Ignoring market construction** — non-contributors get penalized AND miss an authority token; double punishment
- **Rushing to 4 workers** — high knowledge = constant desertions; stabilize at 3 first
- **Hoarding artifact cards** — cards only matter if you complete sets; if morale drops, you discard anyway
- **Neglecting morale** — low morale = tiny hand size = can't hold artifact sets = lost scoring path
- **Not watching opponents' tokens** — count stars placed; if someone has 8-9 down, block their path or race to finish

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Euphoria

**After Setup**: You should have 2 worker dice (rolled, numbers visible), 10 authority tokens, markers on knowledge and morale tracks, 1 face-up recruit, 1 face-down recruit, and artifact cards. Goal: place all 10 authority tokens first.

**Turn 1 — Place a Worker**
- Look at your face-up recruit's faction
- Place a worker on a commodity generation space in that faction's territory
- Gain water, energy, food, or bliss depending on the faction

**Turn 2 — Place Your Other Worker**
- Place on another commodity space, or try a resource market to convert commodities into gold, stone, or clay
- You're stockpiling materials for the mid-game

**Turn 3 — Retrieve Workers**
- Both workers are out, so retrieve them and roll
- **Knowledge check** — add both dice values + knowledge track position; under 16 = safe (example: roll 3 + 5 with knowledge at 4 = 12, well under threshold)

**Turns 4-6 — Build Your Engine**
- Generate more commodities, visit resource markets to produce stone or clay
- Contribute a resource to a market construction site — when it finishes, you place an authority token (1 of 10 done)

**Mid-Game — Get a 3rd Worker**
- Head to the Worker Activation Tank; pay 3 Water (+2 morale, preferred) or 3 Energy (-2 morale)
- Roll the new die and add to your supply — 3 placements per cycle instead of 2
- Be careful: 3 dice means higher knowledge check totals on retrieval

**Mid-to-Late Game — Multiple Paths**
- Contribute to markets for authority tokens + penalty immunity
- Place workers in tunnels close to completion
- Collect and turn in artifact card sets
- Work toward face-down recruit's faction allegiance — Tier 3 flips it for free

**Endgame — Race to 10**
- At 7-8 tokens placed, every turn matters
- Scan for fastest remaining path: tunnel completion, artifact set, market finishing

**Victory**: Place your 10th authority token to win immediately — no waiting for the round to finish.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Knowledge Management
- **Threshold: 16** — available dice values + knowledge track position; stay just below while maximizing worker count
- **Sweet spot** — knowledge track at 3-4 with 3 workers; room for dice totals up to 12-13 without desertion; at knowledge 5+ with 3 workers, even average rolls trigger desertions
- **Bump opponents strategically** — every bump forces a roll and check; borderline knowledge totals can cost them a worker while you take the space's benefit
- **Selective retrieval** — retrieve only some workers; leaving a high-knowledge die on the board keeps it out of your check total

#### Morale Optimization
- **Morale = hand size** — at morale 1-2, barely hold a single set; at morale 5-6, enough to complete sets flexibly
- **Water path for Worker Activation** (+2 morale) always preferred over Energy path (-2 morale); plan commodity generation accordingly
- **Ethical Dilemma timing** — "Fight the Establishment" (new hidden recruit) is powerful if Tier 3 allegiance is close; "Contribute to Dystopia" (direct authority star) is better in the endgame race

#### Faction-Specific Tactics
- **Euphorians** — strongest artifact generation pipeline; pair recruits for chained artifact effects accelerating set completion
- **Wastelanders** — dominate resource production; two recruits fuel rapid market construction, locking down stars while penalizing others
- **Subterrans** — control tunnel networks; tunnel completion places authority tokens AND returns workers (strong tempo); recruits amplify tunnel bonuses
- **Icarites** — excel at track manipulation (knowledge down, morale up); pre-built markets skip construction, giving market actions without building costs

#### Authority Token Acceleration
- **Markets are best value** — each gives a star AND inflicts penalties on non-contributors; in 5-6 player games, being shut out of 2-3 markets creates devastating cumulative penalties
- **Time market completion** — finish when opponents are low on resources so they can't contribute, maximizing penalty spread
- **Tunnel coordination** — place early to reserve your spot; if nobody joins, retrieve and redeploy rather than sitting idle
- **Allegiance Tier 4** — places tokens on ALL active recruits of that faction; with 2 active recruits (original + flipped hidden), that's 2 stars from a single tier trigger

#### Doubles as a Weapon
- **With 3+ dice** — significant odds of rolling doubles, letting you place 2 workers on one turn
- **Retrieve all workers at once** to maximize doubles chance
- **Use doubles offensively** — bump an opponent off a space, then use second placement on the cleared or adjacent high-value space
- Don't plan around doubles, but always check at the start of your turn
